title: Mastery Exam: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking
summary: Mastery Exam: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking retrieves prior skills in an original independent positioning and obstruction decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css normal flow, css floats, css relative position, css absolute position, css fixed sticky, css stacking contexts in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent positioning and obstruction decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck positioning work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the flow, containing-block, and layer system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: shift deck layout enhancement baseline · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent positioning and obstruction decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent positioning and obstruction decision.

content.title: independent positioning and obstruction decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 1: professional layouts require positioning every major region explicitly.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 1: build a readable source-ordered flow baseline first and remove an element from flow only for a relationship that flow cannot express.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 1: absolutely position every heading card and action so browser flow cannot affect the design.

options.text: independent positioning and obstruction decision phase 2.1: confirm the relationship is media wrapped by inline prose
independent positioning and obstruction decision phase 2.2: choose inline-start or inline-end float and bounded size
independent positioning and obstruction decision phase 2.3: contain the float or clear only the required following content
independent positioning and obstruction decision phase 2.4: test short long narrow zoomed and no-image states

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 3: relative offsets cause following siblings to move into the vacated space.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 3: distinguish the original flow box from painted offset, use small local adjustments, and establish containing blocks explicitly.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 3: use relative top offsets to create all vertical spacing between sections.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 4: absolutely positioned content always uses the viewport as its reference.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 5: sticky positioning works independently of ancestor overflow and scroll containers.

Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 5: map scroll containers and containing blocks, use logical offsets and safe-area padding, and test obstruction at zoom plus short and long content.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 5: set every ancestor overflow hidden so sticky knows which box to follow.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 6: the numerically largest z-index must appear above every page element.

Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 6: draw context ancestors and sibling relationships, compare context paint order, and remove accidental boundaries rather than escalating numbers.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 6: add opacity 0.999 to every component to simplify global layer control.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 8: floats are a general two-dimensional page-layout system equivalent to Grid.

Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 8: clear every paragraph so text never wraps beside floated media.

Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 28: identify and establish the intended containing block, reserve real flow space, and test long text zoom and changed card size.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 28: absolutely position every fact row so cards maintain equal height.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 29: sticky positioning works independently of ancestor overflow and scroll containers.

Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 29: map scroll containers and containing blocks, use logical offsets and safe-area padding, and test obstruction at zoom plus short and long content.
Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 29: set every ancestor overflow hidden so sticky knows which box to follow.

options.text: Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 30: the numerically largest z-index must appear above every page element.

Independent positioning and obstruction decision case 30: draw context ancestors and sibling relationships, compare context paint order, and remove accidental boundaries rather than escalating numbers.
